(T04.09)\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport os\nimport re\nimport shutil\nimport threading\nfrom concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom ..pipeline.models import PipelineConfig\nfrom ..pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess\nfrom .models import Finding\nfrom .remediate_prompts import build_remediation_prompt\n\n_log = logging.getLogger(\"superclaude.roadmap.remediate_executor\")\n\n# T04.04: File allowlist per spec section 2.3.5\nEDITABLE_FILES = frozenset({\"roadmap.md\", \"extraction.md\", \"test-strategy.md\"})\n\n# T04.06: Timeout per agent (seconds) per NFR-001\n_AGENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 300\n\n# T04.06: Retry limit per NFR-002\n_AGENT_RETRY_LIMIT = 1\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.03 -- Pre-Remediate File Snapshots\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef create_snapshots(target_files: list[str]) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Create .pre-remediate snapshot copies for all target files.\n\n    Uses atomic copy pattern: read -> write to tmp -> os.replace() per NFR-005.\n    Returns list of snapshot paths for rollback reference.\n\n    Raises FileNotFoundError if a target file does not exist.\n    \"\"\"\n    snapshot_paths: list[str] = []\n    for file_path in target_files:\n        src = Path(file_path)\n        if not src.exists():\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Target file not found: {file_path}\")\n\n        snapshot = Path(f\"{file_path}.pre-remediate\")\n        tmp = Path(f\"{file_path}.pre-remediate.tmp\")\n\n        # Atomic copy: read -> tmp -> os.replace\n        content = src.read_bytes()\n        tmp.write_bytes(content)\n        os.replace(str(tmp), str(snapshot))\n\n        snapshot_paths.append(str(snapshot))\n        _log.info(\"Snapshot created: %s -> %s\", file_path, snapshot)\n\n    return snapshot_paths\n\n\ndef restore_from_snapshots(target_files: list[str]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Restore all target files from their .pre-remediate snapshots.\n\n    Uses os.replace() for atomicity per NFR-005.\n    \"\"\"\n    for file_path in target_files:\n        snapshot = Path(f\"{file_path}.pre-remediate\")\n        if snapshot.exists():\n            os.replace(str(snapshot), str(file_path))\n            _log.info(\"Restored: %s from snapshot\", file_path)\n        else:\n            _log.warning(\"Snapshot not found for %s, cannot restore\", file_path)\n\n\ndef cleanup_snapshots(target_files: list[str]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Delete .pre-remediate snapshots after successful remediation.\"\"\"\n    for file_path in target_files:\n        snapshot = Path(f\"{file_path}.pre-remediate\")\n        if snapshot.exists():\n            snapshot.unlink()\n            _log.info(\"Snapshot cleaned up: %s\", snapshot)\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.04 -- File Allowlist Enforcement\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef enforce_allowlist(\n    findings: list[Finding],\n) -> tuple[list[Finding], list[Finding]]:\n    \"\"\"Enforce file allowlist on findings.\n\n    Findings with ALL files_affected in EDITABLE_FILES pass through.\n    Findings with ANY file outside EDITABLE_FILES are SKIPPED with WARNING.\n\n    Returns (allowed, rejected) tuple.\n\n    Pure function: no I/O, no subprocess, no side effects (NFR-004).\n    \"\"\"\n    allowed: list[Finding] = []\n    rejected: list[Finding] = []\n\n    for finding in findings:\n        if not finding.files_affected:\n            _log.warning(\n                \"Finding %s has no files_affected, SKIPPING\",\n                finding.id,\n            )\n            rejected.append(finding)\n            continue\n\n        # Check if any file is outside the allowlist\n        non_allowed = [\n            f for f in finding.files_affected\n            if _basename(f) not in EDITABLE_FILES\n        ]\n\n        if non_allowed:\n            _log.warning(\n                \"Finding %s references non-allowlisted files: %s -- SKIPPING\",\n                finding.id,\n                \", \".join(non_allowed),\n            )\n            rejected.append(finding)\n        else:\n            allowed.append(finding)\n\n    return allowed, rejected\n\n\ndef _basename(path: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Extract basename from a path, handling both absolute and relative paths.\"\"\"\n    return Path(path).name\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.05 -- Parallel Agent Execution with ClaudeProcess\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef _run_agent_for_file(\n    target_file: str,\n    findings: list[Finding],\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    output_dir: Path,\n) -> tuple[str, int]:\n    \"\"\"Spawn a ClaudeProcess for a single file group.\n\n    Returns (target_file, exit_code) tuple.\n    \"\"\"\n    prompt = build_remediation_prompt(target_file, findings)\n    output_file = output_dir / f\"remediate-{Path(target_file).stem}.md\"\n    error_file = output_file.with_suffix(\".err\")\n\n    proc = ClaudeProcess(\n        prompt=prompt,\n        output_file=output_file,\n        error_file=error_file,\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=_AGENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n        output_format=\"text\",\n        extra_args=[\"--file\", target_file],\n    )\n\n    proc.start()\n    exit_code = proc.wait()\n    return target_file, exit_code\n\n\ndef _run_agent_with_retry(\n    target_file: str,\n    findings: list[Finding],\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    output_dir: Path,\n    all_target_files: list[str],\n) -> tuple[str, int, int]:\n    \"\"\"Run agent with single retry on failure per NFR-002.\n\n    On first failure, restores snapshot and retries once.\n    Returns (target_file, exit_code, attempt_count).\n    \"\"\"\n    # Attempt 1\n    target, exit_code = _run_agent_for_file(\n        target_file, findings, config, output_dir\n    )\n\n    if exit_code == 0:\n        return target, exit_code, 1\n\n    _log.warning(\n        \"Agent for %s failed (exit %d), restoring snapshot and retrying\",\n        target_file,\n        exit_code,\n    )\n\n    # Restore snapshot before retry\n    snapshot = Path(f\"{target_file}.pre-remediate\")\n    if snapshot.exists():\n        os.replace(str(snapshot), str(target_file))\n        # Re-create snapshot for the retry\n        content = Path(target_file).read_bytes()\n        tmp = Path(f\"{target_file}.pre-remediate.tmp\")\n        tmp.write_bytes(content)\n        os.replace(str(tmp), str(snapshot))\n\n    # Attempt 2\n    target, exit_code = _run_agent_for_file(\n        target_file, findings, config, output_dir\n    )\n\n    return target, exit_code, 2\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.07 -- Failure Handling with Full Rollback\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef _handle_failure(\n    failed_file: str,\n    all_target_files: list[str],\n    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n    executor: ThreadPoolExecutor | None = None,\n) -> list[Finding]:\n    \"\"\"Handle agent failure: rollback ALL files, mark findings FAILED.\n\n    Spec section 2.3.8 five-step procedure:\n    1. Halt remaining agents (via executor shutdown)\n    2. Rollback ALL target files from .pre-remediate snapshots\n    3. Mark all findings for failed agent as FAILED\n    4. Mark cross-file findings involving failed file as FAILED\n    5. Set remediate step to FAIL\n\n    Returns list of all findings with updated statuses.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Step 1: Halt remaining agents\n    if executor is not None:\n        executor.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)\n\n    # Step 2: Rollback ALL target files\n    restore_from_snapshots(all_target_files)\n    _log.info(\"Rolled back all %d target files\", len(all_target_files))\n\n    # Steps 3-4: Mark findings as FAILED\n    all_findings: list[Finding] = []\n    for file_path, file_findings in findings_by_file.items():\n        for finding in file_findings:\n            if finding.id not in {f.id for f in all_findings}:\n                # Check if this finding involves the failed file\n                if failed_file in finding.files_affected or file_path == failed_file:\n                    finding.status = \"FAILED\"\n                all_findings.append(finding)\n\n    return all_findings\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.08 -- Success Handling with Snapshot Cleanup\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef _handle_success(\n    all_target_files: list[str],\n    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n) -> list[Finding]:\n    \"\"\"Handle full success: delete snapshots, mark findings FIXED.\n\n    Only runs when ALL agents succeed (not partial).\n    SKIPPED findings remain in SKIPPED status.\n\n    Returns list of all findings with updated statuses.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Delete snapshots\n    cleanup_snapshots(all_target_files)\n\n    # Mark all agent-targeted findings as FIXED (preserve SKIPPED)\n    all_findings: list[Finding] = []\n    seen_ids: set[str] = set()\n    for file_findings in findings_by_file.values():\n        for finding in file_findings:\n            if finding.id not in seen_ids:\n                seen_ids.add(finding.id)\n                if finding.status == \"PENDING\":\n                    finding.status = \"FIXED\"\n                # SKIPPED findings stay SKIPPED\n                all_findings.append(finding)\n\n    return all_findings\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.09 -- Tasklist Outcome Writer (Two-Write Model)\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef update_remediation_tasklist(\n    tasklist_path: str,\n    findings: list[Finding],\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Update existing remediation-tasklist.md with per-finding outcomes.\n\n    This is the second write of the two-write model (first write in T03.04).\n    Updates finding statuses and checkboxes, writes back atomically.\n\n    Uses atomic write (tmp + os.replace) per NFR-005.\n    \"\"\"\n    path = Path(tasklist_path)\n    content = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n    # Build status lookup\n    status_map: dict[str, str] = {f.id: f.status for f in findings}\n\n    # Update frontmatter counts\n    content = _update_frontmatter_counts(content, findings)\n\n    # Update finding entries\n    content = _update_finding_entries(content, status_map)\n\n    # Atomic write\n    tmp = path.with_suffix(path.suffix + \".tmp\")\n    tmp.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    os.replace(str(tmp), str(path))\n\n    _log.info(\"Updated remediation tasklist: %s\", tasklist_path)\n\n\ndef _update_frontmatter_counts(content: str, findings: list[Finding]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Update YAML frontmatter counts based on final statuses.\"\"\"\n    actionable = sum(1 for f in findings if f.status in (\"FIXED\", \"FAILED\", \"PENDING\"))\n    skipped = sum(1 for f in findings if f.status == \"SKIPPED\")\n\n    content = re.sub(\n        r\"^actionable: \\d+\",\n        f\"actionable: {actionable}\",\n        content,\n        flags=re.MULTILINE,\n    )\n    content = re.sub(\n        r\"^skipped: \\d+\",\n        f\"skipped: {skipped}\",\n        content,\n        flags=re.MULTILINE,\n    )\n    return content\n\n\ndef _update_finding_entries(content: str, status_map: dict[str, str]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Update checklist entries with final statuses.\"\"\"\n    for finding_id, status in status_map.items():\n        if status == \"FIXED\":\n            # Change - [ ] F-XX | file | STATUS to - [x] F-XX | file | FIXED\n            content = re.sub(\n                rf\"^(- )\\[ \\]( {re.escape(finding_id)} \\|[^|]*\\|)\\s*\\w+(\\s*--)\",\n                rf\"\\1[x]\\2 FIXED\\3\",\n                content,\n                flags=re.MULTILINE,\n            )\n        elif status == \"FAILED\":\n            # Keep - [ ] but update status to FAILED\n            content = re.sub(\n                rf\"^(- \\[ \\] {re.escape(finding_id)} \\|[^|]*\\|)\\s*\\w+(\\s*--)\",\n                rf\"\\1 FAILED\\2\",\n                content,\n                flags=re.MULTILINE,\n            )\n        # Create snapshots (T04.03)\n    2. Spawn parallel agents (T04.05) with timeout/retry (T04.06)\n    3. On any failure: full rollback (T04.07), return (\"FAIL\", findings)\n    4. On full success: cleanup snapshots (T04.08), return (\"PASS\", findings)\n\n    Returns (status, findings) where status is \"PASS\" or \"FAIL\".\n    \"\"\"\n    all_target_files = list(findings_by_file.keys())\n\n    # Step 1: Create snapshots\n    create_snapshots(all_target_files)\n\n    # Step 2: Spawn parallel agents\n    results: dict[str, tuple[int, int]] = {}  # file -> (exit_code, attempts)\n\n    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=len(all_target_files)) as executor:\n        futures = {}\n        for target_file, file_findings in findings_by_file.items():\n            future = executor.submit(\n                _run_agent_with_retry,\n                target_file,\n                file_findings,\n                config,\n                output_dir,\n                all_target_files,\n            )\n            futures[future] = target_file\n\n        for future in as_completed(futures):\n            target_file = futures[future]\n            try:\n                file_path, exit_code, attempts = future.result()\n                results[file_path] = (exit_code, attempts)\n\n                if exit_code != 0:\n                    # Step 3: Failure -- rollback ALL files\n                    _log.error(\n                        \"Agent for %s failed after %d attempt(s), triggering rollback\",\n                        file_path,\n                        attempts,\n                    )\n                    failed_findings = _handle_failure(\n                        file_path,\n                        all_target_files,\n                        findings_by_file,\n                        executor,\n                    )\n                    return \"FAIL\", failed_findings\n\n            except Exception as exc:\n                _log.error(\"Agent for %s raised exception: %s\", target_file, exc)\n                failed_findings = _handle_failure(\n                    target_file,\n                    all_target_files,\n                    findings_by_file,\n                    executor,\n                )\n                return \"FAIL\", failed_findings\n\n    # Step 4: All agents succeeded\n    success_findings = _handle_success(all_target_files, findings_by_file)\n    return \"PASS\", success_findings\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":36,"oldLines":7,"newStart":36,"newLines":12,"lines":[" # (T04.03)\n- EDITABLE_FILES constant and enforce_allowlist(findings)  (T04.04)\n- execute_remediation(file_groups, config) -> dict  (T04.05)\n- Timeout/retry wrapper  (T04.06)\n- Failure handler with full rollback  (T04.07)\n- Success handler with snapshot cleanup  (T04.08)\n- update_remediation_tasklist(tasklist_path, findings)  (T04.09)\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport os\nimport re\nimport shutil\nimport threading\nfrom concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nfrom ..pipeline.models import PipelineConfig\nfrom ..pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess\nfrom .models import Finding\nfrom .remediate_prompts import build_remediation_prompt\n\n_log = logging.getLogger(\"superclaude.roadmap.remediate_executor\")\n\n# T04.04: File allowlist per spec section 2.3.5\nEDITABLE_FILES = frozenset({\"roadmap.md\", \"extraction.md\", \"test-strategy.md\"})\n\n# T04.06: Timeout per agent (seconds) per NFR-001\n_AGENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 300\n\n# T04.06: Retry limit per NFR-002\n_AGENT_RETRY_LIMIT = 1\n\n# Inline embedding size limit (mirrors executor.py; --file is broken per Phase 1)\n_MAX_ARG_STRLEN = 128 * 1024\n_PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD = 8 * 1024\n_EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT = _MAX_ARG_STRLEN - _PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.03 -- Pre-Remediate File Snapshots\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef create_snapshots(target_files: list[str]) -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Create .pre-remediate snapshot copies for all target files.\n\n    Uses atomic copy pattern: read -> write to tmp -> os.replace() per NFR-005.\n    Returns list of snapshot paths for rollback reference.\n\n    Raises FileNotFoundError if a target file does not exist.\n    \"\"\"\n    snapshot_paths: list[str] = []\n    for file_path in target_files:\n        src = Path(file_path)\n        if not src.exists():\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Target file not found: {file_path}\")\n\n        snapshot = Path(f\"{file_path}.pre-remediate\")\n        tmp = Path(f\"{file_path}.pre-remediate.tmp\")\n\n        # Atomic copy: read -> tmp -> os.replace\n        content = src.read_bytes()\n        tmp.write_bytes(content)\n        os.replace(str(tmp), str(snapshot))\n\n        snapshot_paths.append(str(snapshot))\n        _log.info(\"Snapshot created: %s -> %s\", file_path, snapshot)\n\n    return snapshot_paths\n\n\ndef restore_from_snapshots(target_files: list[str]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Restore all target files from their .pre-remediate snapshots.\n\n    Uses os.replace() for atomicity per NFR-005.\n    \"\"\"\n    for file_path in target_files:\n        snapshot = Path(f\"{file_path}.pre-remediate\")\n        if snapshot.exists():\n            os.replace(str(snapshot), str(file_path))\n            _log.info(\"Restored: %s from snapshot\", file_path)\n        else:\n            _log.warning(\"Snapshot not found for %s, cannot restore\", file_path)\n\n\ndef cleanup_snapshots(target_files: list[str]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Delete .pre-remediate snapshots after successful remediation.\"\"\"\n    for file_path in target_files:\n        snapshot = Path(f\"{file_path}.pre-remediate\")\n        if snapshot.exists():\n            snapshot.unlink()\n            _log.info(\"Snapshot cleaned up: %s\", snapshot)\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.04 -- File Allowlist Enforcement\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef enforce_allowlist(\n    findings: list[Finding],\n) -> tuple[list[Finding], list[Finding]]:\n    \"\"\"Enforce file allowlist on findings.\n\n    Findings with ALL files_affected in EDITABLE_FILES pass through.\n    Findings with ANY file outside EDITABLE_FILES are SKIPPED with WARNING.\n\n    Returns (allowed, rejected) tuple.\n\n    Pure function: no I/O, no subprocess, no side effects (NFR-004).\n    \"\"\"\n    allowed: list[Finding] = []\n    rejected: list[Finding] = []\n\n    for finding in findings:\n        if not finding.files_affected:\n            _log.warning(\n                \"Finding %s has no files_affected, SKIPPING\",\n                finding.id,\n            )\n            rejected.append(finding)\n            continue\n\n        # Check if any file is outside the allowlist\n        non_allowed = [\n            f for f in finding.files_affected\n            if _basename(f) not in EDITABLE_FILES\n        ]\n\n        if non_allowed:\n            _log.warning(\n                \"Finding %s references non-allowlisted files: %s -- SKIPPING\",\n                finding.id,\n                \", \".join(non_allowed),\n            )\n            rejected.append(finding)\n        else:\n            allowed.append(finding)\n\n    return allowed, rejected\n\n\ndef _basename(path: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Extract basename from a path, handling both absolute and relative paths.\"\"\"\n    return Path(path).name\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.05 -- Parallel Agent Execution with ClaudeProcess\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef _run_agent_for_file(\n    target_file: str,\n    findings: list[Finding],\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    output_dir: Path,\n) -> tuple[str, int]:\n    \"\"\"Spawn a ClaudeProcess for a single file group.\n\n    Returns (target_file, exit_code) tuple.\n    \"\"\"\n    prompt = build_remediation_prompt(target_file, findings)\n    output_file = output_dir / f\"remediate-{Path(target_file).stem}.md\"\n    error_file = output_file.with_suffix(\".err\")\n\n    proc = ClaudeProcess(\n        prompt=prompt,\n        output_file=output_file,\n        error_file=error_file,\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=_AGENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,\n        output_format=\"text\",\n        extra_args=[\"--file\", target_file],\n    )\n\n    proc.start()\n    exit_code = proc.wait()\n    return target_file, exit_code\n\n\ndef _run_agent_with_retry(\n    target_file: str,\n    findings: list[Finding],\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    output_dir: Path,\n    all_target_files: list[str],\n) -> tuple[str, int, int]:\n    \"\"\"Run agent with single retry on failure per NFR-002.\n\n    On first failure, restores snapshot and retries once.\n    Returns (target_file, exit_code, attempt_count).\n    \"\"\"\n    # Attempt 1\n    target, exit_code = _run_agent_for_file(\n        target_file, findings, config, output_dir\n    )\n\n    if exit_code == 0:\n        return target, exit_code, 1\n\n    _log.warning(\n        \"Agent for %s failed (exit %d), restoring snapshot and retrying\",\n        target_file,\n        exit_code,\n    )\n\n    # Restore snapshot before retry\n    snapshot = Path(f\"{target_file}.pre-remediate\")\n    if snapshot.exists():\n        os.replace(str(snapshot), str(target_file))\n        # Re-create snapshot for the retry\n        content = Path(target_file).read_bytes()\n        tmp = Path(f\"{target_file}.pre-remediate.tmp\")\n        tmp.write_bytes(content)\n        os.replace(str(tmp), str(snapshot))\n\n    # Attempt 2\n    target, exit_code = _run_agent_for_file(\n        target_file, findings, config, output_dir\n    )\n\n    return target, exit_code, 2\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.07 -- Failure Handling with Full Rollback\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef _handle_failure(\n    failed_file: str,\n    all_target_files: list[str],\n    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n    executor: ThreadPoolExecutor | None = None,\n) -> list[Finding]:\n    \"\"\"Handle agent failure: rollback ALL files, mark findings FAILED.\n\n    Spec section 2.3.8 five-step procedure:\n    1. Halt remaining agents (via executor shutdown)\n    2. Rollback ALL target files from .pre-remediate snapshots\n    3. Mark all findings for failed agent as FAILED\n    4. Mark cross-file findings involving failed file as FAILED\n    5. Set remediate step to FAIL\n\n    Returns list of all findings with updated statuses.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Step 1: Halt remaining agents\n    if executor is not None:\n        executor.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)\n\n    # Step 2: Rollback ALL target files\n    restore_from_snapshots(all_target_files)\n    _log.info(\"Rolled back all %d target files\", len(all_target_files))\n\n    # Steps 3-4: Mark findings as FAILED\n    all_findings: list[Finding] = []\n    for file_path, file_findings in findings_by_file.items():\n        for finding in file_findings:\n            if finding.id not in {f.id for f in all_findings}:\n                # Check if this finding involves the failed file\n                if failed_file in finding.files_affected or file_path == failed_file:\n                    finding.status = \"FAILED\"\n                all_findings.append(finding)\n\n    return all_findings\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.08 -- Success Handling with Snapshot Cleanup\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef _handle_success(\n    all_target_files: list[str],\n    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n) -> list[Finding]:\n    \"\"\"Handle full success: delete snapshots, mark findings FIXED.\n\n    Only runs when ALL agents succeed (not partial).\n    SKIPPED findings remain in SKIPPED status.\n\n    Returns list of all findings with updated statuses.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Delete snapshots\n    cleanup_snapshots(all_target_files)\n\n    # Mark all agent-targeted findings as FIXED (preserve SKIPPED)\n    all_findings: list[Finding] = []\n    seen_ids: set[str] = set()\n    for file_findings in findings_by_file.values():\n        for finding in file_findings:\n            if finding.id not in seen_ids:\n                seen_ids.add(finding.id)\n                if finding.status == \"PENDING\":\n                    finding.status = \"FIXED\"\n                # SKIPPED findings stay SKIPPED\n                all_findings.append(finding)\n\n    return all_findings\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.09 -- Tasklist Outcome Writer (Two-Write Model)\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef update_remediation_tasklist(\n    tasklist_path: str,\n    findings: list[Finding],\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Update existing remediation-tasklist.md with per-finding outcomes.\n\n    This is the second write of the two-write model (first write in T03.04).\n    Updates finding statuses and checkboxes, writes back atomically.\n\n    Uses atomic write (tmp + os.replace) per NFR-005.\n    \"\"\"\n    path = Path(tasklist_path)\n    content = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n    # Build status lookup\n    status_map: dict[str, str] = {f.id: f.status for f in findings}\n\n    # Update frontmatter counts\n    content = _update_frontmatter_counts(content, findings)\n\n    # Update finding entries\n    content = _update_finding_entries(content, status_map)\n\n    # Atomic write\n    tmp = path.with_suffix(path.suffix + \".tmp\")\n    tmp.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    os.replace(str(tmp), str(path))\n\n    _log.info(\"Updated remediation tasklist: %s\", tasklist_path)\n\n\ndef _update_frontmatter_counts(content: str, findings: list[Finding]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Update YAML frontmatter counts based on final statuses.\"\"\"\n    actionable = sum(1 for f in findings if f.status in (\"FIXED\", \"FAILED\", \"PENDING\"))\n    skipped = sum(1 for f in findings if f.status == \"SKIPPED\")\n\n    content = re.sub(\n        r\"^actionable: \\d+\",\n        f\"actionable: {actionable}\",\n        content,\n        flags=re.MULTILINE,\n    )\n    content = re.sub(\n        r\"^skipped: \\d+\",\n        f\"skipped: {skipped}\",\n        content,\n        flags=re.MULTILINE,\n    )\n    return content\n\n\ndef _update_finding_entries(content: str, status_map: dict[str, str]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Update checklist entries with final statuses.\"\"\"\n    for finding_id, status in status_map.items():\n        if status == \"FIXED\":\n            # Change - [ ] F-XX | file | STATUS to - [x] F-XX | file | FIXED\n            content = re.sub(\n                rf\"^(- )\\[ \\]( {re.escape(finding_id)} \\|[^|]*\\|)\\s*\\w+(\\s*--)\",\n                rf\"\\1[x]\\2 FIXED\\3\",\n                content,\n                flags=re.MULTILINE,\n            )\n        elif status == \"FAILED\":\n            # Keep - [ ] but update status to FAILED\n            content = re.sub(\n                rf\"^(- \\[ \\] {re.escape(finding_id)} \\|[^|]*\\|)\\s*\\w+(\\s*--)\",\n                rf\"\\1 FAILED\\2\",\n                content,\n                flags=re.MULTILINE,\n            )\n        # SKIPPED entries already have [x] from T03.04, leave unchanged\n\n    return content\n\n\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n# T04.05 + T04.07 + T04.08 -- Main Execution Coordinator\n# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n\ndef execute_remediation(\n    findings_by_file: dict[str, list[Finding]],\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    output_dir: Path,\n) -> tuple[str, list[Finding]]:\n    \"\"\"Execute remediation agents in parallel across file groups.\n\n    Orchestrates:\n    1. Create snapshots (T04.03)\n    2. Spawn parallel agents (T04.05) with timeout/retry (T04.06)\n    3. On any failure: full rollback (T04.07), return (\"FAIL\", findings)\n    4. On full success: cleanup snapshots (T04.08), return (\"PASS\", findings)\n\n    Returns (status, findings) where status is \"PASS\" or \"FAIL\".\n    \"\"\"\n    all_target_files = list(findings_by_file.keys())\n\n    # Step 1: Create snapshots\n    create_snapshots(all_target_files)\n\n    # Step 2: Spawn parallel agents\n    results: dict[str, tuple[int, int]] = {}  # file -> (exit_code, attempts)\n\n    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=len(all_target_files)) as executor:\n        futures = {}\n        for target_file, file_findings in findings_by_file.items():\n            future = executor.submit(\n                _run_agent_with_retry,\n                target_file,\n                file_findings,\n                config,\n                output_dir,\n                all_target_files,\n            )\n            futures[future] = target_file\n\n        for future in as_completed(futures):\n            target_file = futures[future]\n            try:\n                file_path, exit_code, attempts = future.result()\n                results[file_path] = (exit_code, attempts)\n\n                if exit_code != 0:\n                    # Step 3: Failure -- rollback ALL files\n                    _log.error(\n                        \"Agent for %s failed after %d attempt(s), triggering rollback\",\n                        file_path,\n                        attempts,\n                    )\n                    failed_findings = _handle_failure(\n                        file_path,\n                        all_target_files,\n                        findings_by_file,\n                        executor,\n                    )\n                    return \"FAIL\", failed_findings\n\n            except Exception as exc:\n                _log.error(\"Agent for %s raised exception: %s\", target_file, exc)\n                failed_findings = _handle_failure(\n                    target_file,\n                    all_target_files,\n                    findings_by_file,\n                    executor,\n                )\n                return \"FAIL\", failed_findings\n\n    # Step 4: All agents succeeded\n    success_findings = _handle_success(all_target_files, findings_by_file)\n    return \"PASS\", success_findings\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":166,"oldLines":7,"newStart":166,"newLines":26,"lines":[" ","     Returns (target_file, exit_code) tuple.","     \"\"\"","-    prompt = build_remediation_prompt(target_file, findings)","+    base_prompt = build_remediation_prompt(target_file, findings)","+","+    # import os\n    import re\n\n    try:\n        content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        return 0\n\n    if content.lstrip().startswith(\"---\"):\n        return 0\n\n    match = re.search(r\"^---[ \\t]*$\", content, re.MULTILINE)\n    if match is None:\n        return 0\n\n    preamble = content[: match.start()]\n    cleaned = content[match.start() :]\n    preamble_bytes = len(preamble.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n\n    tmp_file = output_file.with_suffix(output_file.suffix + \".tmp\")\n    tmp_file.write_text(cleaned, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    os.replace(tmp_file, output_file)\n\n    _log.info(\"Stripped %d-byte preamble from %s\", preamble_bytes, output_file)\n    return preamble_bytes\n\n\ndef validate_run_step(\n    step: Step,\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\n) -> StepResult:\n    \"\"\"Execute a single validation step as a Claude subprocess.\n\n    Mirrors ``roadmap_run_step`` from ``executor.py``: builds argv,\n    launches process, waits with timeout, sanitizes output.\n    \"\"\"\n    started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n    if config.dry_run:\n        _log.info(\"[dry-run] Would execute step '%s'\", step.id)\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.PASS,\n            attempt=1,\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n        )\n\n    # Inline embedding\n    embedded = _embed_inputs(step.inputs)\n    if embedded and len(embedded.encode(\"utf-8\")) <= _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT:\n        effective_prompt = step.prompt + \"\\n\\n\" + embedded\n        extra_args: list[str] = []\n    elif embedded:\n        effective_prompt = step.prompt\n        extra_args = [\n            arg\n            for input_path in step.inputs\n            for arg in (\"--file\", str(input_path))\n        ]\n    else:\n        effective_prompt error_file=step.output_file.with_suffix(\".err\"),\n        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n        model=step.model or config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=step.timeout_seconds,\n        output_format=\"text\",\n        extra_args=extra_args,\n    )\n\n    proc.start()\n\n    while proc._process is not None and proc._process.poll() is None:\n        if cancel_check():\n            proc.terminate()\n            return StepResult(\n                step=step,\n                status=StepStatus.CANCELLED,\n                attempt=1,\n                gate_failure_reason=\"Cancelled by external signal\",\n                started_at=started_at,\n                finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n            )\n        time.sleep(1)\n\n    exit_code = proc.wait()\n    finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.TIMEOUT,\n            attempt=1,\n            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' timed out after {step.timeout_seconds}s\",\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=finished_at,\n        )\n\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n            attempt=1,\n            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' exited with code {exit_code}\",\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=finished_at,\n        )\n\n    _sanitize_output(step.output_file)\n\n    return StepResult(\n        step=step,\n        status=StepStatus.PASS,\n        attempt=1,\n        started_at=started_at,\n        finished_at=finished_at,\n    )\n\n\ndef _validate_input_files(output_dir: Path) -> list[Path]:\n    \"\"\"Validate that required input files exist, return their paths.\n\n    Raises FileNotFoundError if any required file is missing.\n    \"\"\"\n    paths = []\n    for name in _REQUIRED_INPUTS:\n        p = output_dir / name\n        if not p.exists():\n            raise FileNotFoundError(\n                f\"Required validation input not found: {p}\"\n            )\n        paths.append(p)\n    return paths\n\n\ndef _build_single_agent_steps(\n    config: ValidateConfig,\n    validate_dir: Path,\n    input_paths: list[Path],\n) -> list[Step | list[Step]]:\n    \"\"\"Build steps for single-agent validation (1 agent -> reflect -> report).\"\"\"\n    roadmap, test_strategy, extraction = input_paths\n\n    return [\n        Step(\n            id=\"reflect\",\n            prompt=build_reflect_prompt(\n                str(roadmap), str(test_strategy), str(extraction),\n            ),\n            output_file=validate_dir / \"validation-report.md\",\n            gate=REFLECT_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=600,\n            inputs=input_paths,\n            retry_limit=1,\n            model=config.agents[0].model,\n        ),\n    ]\n\n\ndef _build_multi_agent_steps(\n    config: ValidateConfig,\n    validate_dir: Path,\n    input_paths: list[Path],\n) -> list[Step | list[Step]]:\n    \"\"\"Build steps for multi-agent validation.\n\n    N agents -> N parallel reflections -> gate each -> adversarial merge.\n    \"\"\"\n    roadmap, test_strategy, extraction = input_paths\n\n    # Parallel reflection steps (one per agent)\n    reflect_steps = []\n    reflect_outputs = []\n    for agent in config.agents:\n        output = validate_dir / f\"reflect-{agent.id}.md\"\n        reflect_outputs.append(output)\n        reflect_steps.append(\n            Step(\n                id=f\"reflect-{agent.id}\",\n                prompt=build_reflect_prompt(\n                    str(roadmap), str(test_strategy), str(extraction),\n                ),\n                output_file=output,\n                gate=REFLECT_GATE,\n                timeout_seconds=600,\n                inputs=input_paths,\n                retry_limit=1,\n                model=agent.model,\n            ),\n        )\n\n    # Adversarial merge step\n    merge_step = Step(\n        id=\"adversarial-merge\",\n        prompt=build_merge_prompt([str(p) for p in reflect_outputs]),\n        output_file=validate_dir / \"validation-report.md\",\n        gate=ADVERSARIAL_MERGE_GATE,\n        timeout_seconds=600,\n        inputs=reflect_outputs,\n        retry_limit=1,\n    )\n\n    return [\n        reflect_steps,  # parallel group\n        merge_step,     # sequential after all reflections\n    ]\n\n\ndef _parse_report_counts(report_path: Path) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Parse blocking/warning/info counts from a validation report's frontmatter.\n\n    Returns dict with blocking_count, warning_count, info_count.\n    Falls back to 0 for any unparseable field.\n    \"\"\"\n    result = {\"blocking_count\": 0, \"warning_count\": 0, \"info_count\": 0}\n\n    if not report_path.exists():\n        return result\n\n    content = report_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return result\n\n    end_idx = content.find(\"\\n---\", 3)\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return result\n\n    frontmatter = content[3:end_idx]\n    for line in frontmatter.splitlines():\n        line = line.strip()\n        if \":\" not in line:\n            continue\n        key, _, val = line.partition(\":\")\n        key = key.strip()\n        val = val.strip()\n        if key == \"blocking_issues_count\":\n            try:\n                result[\"blocking_count\"] = int(val)\n            except ValueError:\n                pass\n        elif key == \"warnings_count\":\n            try:\n                result[\"warning_count\"] = int(val)\n            except ValueError:\n                pass\n\n    # Count INFO findings from body\n    body = content[end_idx + 4:]  # skip closing ---\n    info_count = 0\n    for line in body.splitlines():\n        if \"[INFO]\" in line:\n            info_count += 1\n    result[\"info_count\"] = info_count\n\n    return result\n\n\ndef _write_degraded_report(\n    report_path: Path,\n    failed_ids: list[str],\n    passed_ids: list[str],\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Write a degraded validation report when multi-agent validation partially fails.\n\n    The report is unmistakably marked as incomplete:\n    - YAML frontmatter with ``validation_complete: false``\n    - Prominent warning banner naming failed agent(s)\n    - Per OQ-2: \"Silent degradation is unacceptable.\"\n    \"\"\"\n    failed_list = \", \".join(failed_ids)\n    passed_list = \", \".join(passed_ids) if passed_ids else \"none\"\n\n    content = (\n        \"---\\n\"\n        \"validation_complete: false\\n\"\n        \"blocking_issues_count: 0\\n\"\n        \"warnings_count: 0\\n\"\n        f\"failed_agents: {failed_list}\\n\"\n        f\"passed_agents: {passed_list}\\n\"\n        \"---\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"> **WARNING: DEGRADED VALIDATION REPORT**\\n\"\n        \">\\n\"\n        f\"> The following validation agent(s) FAILED: **{failed_list}**\\n\"\n        \">\\n\"\n        \"> This report is INCOMPLETE. Findings from failed agents are missing.\\n\"\n        \"> Re-run validation to obtain a complete report.\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"## Status\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"This is a degraded validation report produced because one or more\\n\"\n        \"validation agents failed during multi-agent validation.\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        f\"- **Failed agents:** {failed_list}\\n\"\n        f\"- **Passed agents:** {passed_list}\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"## Findings\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"No consolidated findings available due to partial agent failure.\\n\"\n        \"Individual reflection files from successful agents (if any) are\\n\"\n        \"preserved in the validate/ directory.\\n\"\n    )\n\n    report_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    report_path.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    _log.warning(\"Wrote degraded validation report to %s\", report_path)\n\n\ndef execute_validate(config: ValidateConfig) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Execute the validation pipeline.\n\n    Reads 3 input files (roadmap.md, test-strategy.md, extraction.md),\n    routes by agent count, applies gates, writes reports.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    config:\n        ValidateConfig with output_dir (containing pipeline outputs)\n        and agents list (1 agent = single mode, N = multi-agent).\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    dict with keys:\n        blocking_count: int -- total BLOCKING findings\n        warning_count: int -- total WARNING findings\n        info_count: int -- total INFO findings\n\n    Raises\n    ------\n    FileNotFoundError\n        If any of the 3 required input files is missing.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Validate inputs exist\n    input_paths = _validate_input_files(config.output_dir)\n\n    # Create validate/ subdirectory\n    validate_dir = config.output_dir / \"validate\"\n    validate_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Route by agent count\n    if len(config.agents) == 1:\n        steps = _build_single_agent_steps(config, validate_dir, input_paths)\n    else:\n        steps = _build_multi_agent_steps(config, validate_dir, input_paths)\n\n    # Execute pipeline\n    results = execute_pipeline(\n        steps=steps,\n        config=config,\n        run_step=validate_run_step,\n    )\n\n    # Check for failures and handle partial failure in multi-agent mode\n    failures = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n    report_path = validate_dir / \"validation-report.md\"\n\n    if failures and len(config.agents) > 1:\n        # Partial failure: some agents may have succeeded\n        failed_ids = [r.step.id for r in failures if r.step]\n        passed_ids = [\n            r.step.id for r in results\n            if r.status == StepStatus.PASS and r.step\n        ]\n        _log.error(\n            \"Validation partial failure: passed=%s, failed=%s\",\n            passed_ids,\n            failed_ids,\n        )\n        _write_degraded_report(report_path, failed_ids, passed_ids)\n    elif failures:\n        _log.error(\n            \"Validation pipeline halted: %d step(s) failed\",\n            len(failures),\n        )\n\n    return _parse_report_counts(report_path)\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":96,"oldLines":18,"newStart":96,"newLines":19,"lines":["             finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),","         )"," ","-    # model=step.model or config.model,\n        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n        timeout_seconds=step.timeout_seconds,\n        output_format=\"text\",\n        extra_args=extra_args,\n    )\n\n    proc.start()\n\n    while proc._process is not None and proc._process.poll() is None:\n        if cancel_check():\n            proc.terminate()\n            return StepResult(\n              finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n            )\n        time.sleep(1)\n\n    exit_code = proc.wait()\n    finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.TIMEOUT,\n            attempt=1,\n            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' timed out after frontmatter.\n\n    Returns dict with blocking_count, warning_count, info_count.\n    Falls back to 0 for any unparseable field.\n    \"\"\"\n    result = {\"blocking_count\": 0, \"warning_count\": 0, \"info_count\": 0}\n\n    if not report_path.exists():\n        return result\n\n    content = report_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return result\n\n    end_idx = content.find(\"\\n---\", 3)\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return result\n\n    frontmatter = content[3:end_idx]\n    for line in frontmatter.splitlines():\n        line = \"blocking_issues_count\":\n            try:\n                result[\"blocking_count\"] = int(val)\n            except ValueError:\n                pass\n        elif key == \"warnings_count\":\n            try:\n                result[\"warning_count\"] = int(val)\n            except ValueError:\n                pass\n\n    # Count INFO findings from body\n    body = content[end_idx + 4:]  # skip closing ---\n    info_count = 0\n    for line in body.splitlines():\n        if \"[INFO]\" in line:\n            info_count += 1\n    result[\"info_count\"] = info_count\n\n    return result\n\n\ndef _write_degraded_report(\n    report_path: Path,\n    failed_ids: list[str],\n    passed_ids: list[str],\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Write a degraded validation report when multi-agent validation partially fails.\n\n    The report is unmistakably marked as incomplete:\n    - YAML frontmatter with ``validation_complete: false``\n    - Prominent warning banner naming failed agent(s)\n    - Per OQ-2: \"Silent degradation is unacceptable.\"\n    \"\"\"\n    failed_list = \", \".join(failed_ids)\n    passed_list = \", \".join(passed_ids) if passed_ids else \"none\"\n\n    content = (\n        \"---\\n\"\n        \"validation_complete: false\\n\"\n        \"blocking_issues_count: 0\\n\"\n        \"warnings_count: 0\\n\"\n        f\"failed_agents: {failed_list}\\n\"\n        f\"passed_agents: {passed_list}\\n\"\n        \"---\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"> **WARNING: DEGRADED VALIDATION REPORT**\\n\"\n        \">\\n\"\n        f\"> The following validation agent(s) FAILED: **{failed_list}**\\n\"\n        \">\\n\"\n        \"> This report is INCOMPLETE. Findings from failed agents are missing.\\n\"\n        \"> Re-run validation to obtain a complete report.\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"## Status\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"This is a degraded validation report produced because one or more\\n\"\n        \"validation agents failed during multi-agent validation.\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        f\"- **Failed agents:** {failed_list}\\n\"\n        f\"- **Passed agents:** {passed_list}\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"## Findings\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"No consolidated findings available due to partial agent failure.\\n\"\n        \"Individual reflection files from successful agents (if any) are\\n\"\n        \"preserved in the validate/ directory.\\n\"\n    )\n\n    report_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    report_path.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    _log.warning(\"Wrote degraded validation report to %s\", report_path)\n\n\ndef execute_validate(config: ValidateConfig) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Execute the validation pipeline.\n\n    Reads 3 input files (roadmap.md, test-strategy.md, extraction.md),\n    routes by agent count, applies gates, writes reports.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    config:\n        ValidateConfig with output_dir (containing pipeline outputs)\n        and agents list (1 agent = single mode, N = multi-agent).\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    dict with keys:\n        blocking_count: int -- total BLOCKING findings\n        warning_count: int -- total WARNING findings\n        info_count: int -- total INFO findings\n\n    Raises\n    ------\n    FileNotFoundError\n        If any of the 3 required input files is missing.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Validate inputs exist\n    input_paths = _validate_input_files(config.output_dir)\n\n    # Create validate/ subdirectory\n    validate_dir = config.output_dir / \"validate\"\n    validate_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Route by agent count\n    if len(config.agents) == 1:\n        steps = _build_single_agent_steps(config, validate_dir, input_paths)\n    else:\n        steps = _build_multi_agent_steps(config, validate_dir, input_paths)\n\n    # Execute pipeline\n    results = execute_pipeline(\n        steps=steps,\n        config=config,\n        run_step=validate_run_step,\n    )\n\n    # Check for failures and handle partial failure in multi-agent mode\n    failures = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n    report_path = validate_dir / \"validation-report.md\"\n\n    if failures and len(config.agents) > 1:\n        # Partial failure: some agents may have succeeded\n        failed_ids = [r.step.id for r in failures if r.step]\n        passed_ids = [\n            r.step.id for r in results\n            if r.status == StepStatus.PASS and r.step\n        ]\n        _log.error(\n            \"Validation partial failure: passed=%s, failed=%s\",\n            passed_ids,\n            failed_ids,\n        )\n        _write_degraded_report(report_path, failed_ids, passed_ids)\n    elif failures:\n        _log.error(\n            \"Validation pipeline halted: %d step(s) failed\",\n            len(failures),\n        )\n\n    return _parse_report_counts(report_path)\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":7,"oldLines":7,"newStart":7,"newLines":8,"lines":[" Reuses ``execute_pipeline()`` and ``ClaudeProcess`` from the pipeline module."," No new subprocess abstractions (per roadmap constraint)."," ","-Context isolation: each subprocess receives only its prompt and --file inputs.","+Context isolation: each subprocess receives only its prompt via inline embedding.","+--file is a cloud download mechanism and does not inject local file content."," \"\"\""," "," from __future__ import annotations"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

Findings from failed agents are missing.\\n\"\n        \"> Re-run validation to obtain a complete report.\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"## Status\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"This is a degraded validation report produced because one or more\\n\"\n        \"validation agents failed during multi-agent validation.\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        f\"- **Failed agents:** {failed_list}\\n\"\n        f\"- **Passed agents:** {passed_list}\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"## Findings\\n\"\n        \"\\n\"\n        \"No consolidated findings available due to partial agent failure.\\n\"\n        \"Individual reflection files from successful agents (if any) are\\n\"\n        \"preserved in the validate/ directory.\\n\"\n    )\n\n    report_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    report_path.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    _log.warning(\"Wrote degraded validation report to %s\", report_path)\n\n\ndef execute_validate(config: ValidateConfig) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Execute the validation pipeline.\n\n    Reads 3 input files (roadmap.md, test-strategy.md, extraction.md),\n    routes by agent count, applies gates, writes reports.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    config:\n        ValidateConfig with output_dir (containing pipeline outputs)\n        and agents list (1 agent = single mode, N = multi-agent).\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    dict with keys:\n        blocking_count: int -- total BLOCKING findings\n        warning_count: int -- total WARNING findings\n        info_count: int -- total INFO findings\n\n    Raises\n    ------\n    FileNotFoundError\n        If any of the 3 required input files is missing.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Validate inputs exist\n    input_paths = _validate_input_files(config.output_dir)\n\n    # Create validate/ subdirectory\n    validate_dir = config.output_dir / \"validate\"\n    validate_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Route by agent count\n    if len(config.agents) == 1:\n        steps = _build_single_agent_steps(config, validate_dir, input_paths)\n    else:\n        steps = _build_multi_agent_steps(config, validate_dir, input_paths)\n\n    # Execute pipeline\n    results = execute_pipeline(\n        steps=steps,\n        config=config,\n        run_step=validate_run_step,\n    )\n\n    # Check for failures and handle partial failure in multi-agent mode\n    failures = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n    report_path = validate_dir / \"validation-report.md\"\n\n    if failures and len(config.agents) > 1:\n        # Partial failure: some agents may have succeeded\n        failed_ids = [r.step.id for r in failures if r.step]\n        passed_ids = [\n            r.step.id for r in results\n            if r.status == StepStatus.PASS and r.step\n        ]\n        _log.error(\n            \"Validation partial failure: passed=%s, failed=%s\",\n            passed_ids,\n            failed_ids,\n        )\n        _write_degraded_report(report_path, failed_ids, passed_ids)\n    elif failures:\n        _log.error(\n            \"Validation pipeline halted: %d step(s) failed\",\n            len(failures),\n        )\n\n    return _parse_report_counts(report_path)\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":28,"oldLines":7,"newStart":28,"newLines":7,"lines":[" "," _log = logging.getLogger(\"superclaude.roadmap.validate_executor\")"," ","-# Threshold above which inline embedding falls back to --file flags","+# Threshold above which inline embedding logs a warning (--file fallback removed)"," _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT = 100 * 1024  # new subprocess abstractions.\n\nContext isolation: each subprocess receives only its prompt and --file inputs.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport os\nimport 1024  # 100 KB\n\n\ndef _collect_tasklist_files(tasklist_dir: Path) -> list[Path]:\n    \"\"\"Collect all markdown files in the tasklist directory.\n\n    Returns sorted list of .md files for deterministic ordering.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not tasklist_dir.is_dir():\n        raise FileNotFoundError(\n            f\"Tasklist directory not found: {tasklist_dir}\"\n        )\n    files = sorted(tasklist_dir.glob(\"*.md\"))\n    if not files:\n        raise FileNotFoundError(\n            f\"No markdown files found in tasklist directory: {tasklist_dir}\"\n        )\n    return files\n\n\ndef _embed_inputs(input_paths: list[Path]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Read input files and return their contents as fenced code blocks.\"\"\"\n    if not input_paths:\n        return \"\"\n    blocks: list[str] = []\n    for p in input_paths:\n        content = Path(p).read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        blocks.append(f\"# {p}\\n```\\n{content}\\n```\")\n    return \"\\n\\n\".join(blocks)\n\n\ndef _sanitize_output(output_file: Path) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Strip conversational preamble before YAML frontmatter.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        return 0\n\n    if content.lstrip().startswith(\"---\"):\n        return 0\n\n    match = re.search(r\"^---[ \\t]*$\", content, re.MULTILINE)\n    if match is None:\n        return 0\n\n    preamble = content[: match.start()]\n    cleaned = content[match.start():]\n    preamble_bytes = ``roadmap/validate_executor.py``.\n    \"\"\"\n    started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n    if config.dry_run:\n        _log.info(\"[dry-run] Would execute step '%s'\", step.id)\n        return finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n            )\n        time.sleep(1)\n\n    exit_code = proc.wait()\n    finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return finished_at=finished_at,\n    )\n\n\ndef _build_steps(config: TasklistValidateConfig) -> list[Step]:\n    \"\"\"Build the tasklist validation pipeline steps.\"\"\"\n    tasklist_files = finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n            )\n        time.sleep(1)\n\n    exit_code = proc.wait()\n    finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.TIMEOUT,\n            attempt=1,\n            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' timed out after {step.timeout_seconds}s\",\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=finished_at,\n        )\n\n    if exit_code != 0:\n        return StepResult(\n            step=step,\n            status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n            attempt=1,\n            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' exited with code {exit_code}\",\n            started_at=started_at,\n            finished_at=finished_at,\n        )\n\n    _sanitize_output(step.output_file)\n\n    return StepResult(\n        step=step,\n        status=StepStatus.PASS,\n        attempt=1,\n        started_at=started_at,\n        finished_at=finished_at,\n    )\n\n\ndef _build_steps(config: TasklistValidateConfig) -> list[Step]:\n    \"\"\"Build the tasklist validation pipeline steps.\"\"\"\n    tasklist_files = _collect_tasklist_files(config.tasklist_dir)\n    all_inputs = [config.roadmap_file] + tasklist_files\n\n    return [\n        Step(\n            id=\"tasklist-fidelity\",\n gate=TASKLIST_FIDELITY_GATE,\n            timeout_seconds=600,\n            inputs=all_inputs,\n            retry_limit=1,\n            model=config.model,\n        ),\n    ]\n\n\ndef _has_high_severity(report_path: Path) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if the fidelity report has HIGH severity deviations.\n\n    Returns True if high_severity_count > 0, used for CLI exit code.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not report_path.exists():\n        return True  # No report = assume failure\n\n    content = report_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    stripped = content.lstrip()\n    if not stripped.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return True\n\n    rest = stripped[3:].lstrip(\"\\n\")\n    end_idx = rest.find(\"\\n---\")\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return True\n\n    for line in rest[:end_idx].splitlines():\n        line = line.strip()\n        if line.startswith(\"high_severity_count:\"):\n            value = line.split(\":\", 1)[1].strip()\n            try:\n                return int(value) > 0\n            except (ValueError, TypeError):\n                return True\n\n    return True  # Field not found = assume failure\n\n\ndef execute_tasklist_validate(config: TasklistValidateConfig) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Execute the tasklist validation pipeline.\n\n    Returns True if validation passed (no HIGH severity deviations),\n    False otherwise (used for CLI exit code).\n    \"\"\"\n    steps = _build_steps(config)\n\n    results = execute_pipeline(\n        steps=steps,\n        config=config,\n        run_step=tasklist_run_step,\n    )\n\n    report_path = config.output_dir / \"tasklist-fidelity.md\"\n\n    # Check for pipeline failures\n    failures = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n    if failures:\n        _log.error(\n            \"Tasklist validation pipeline halted: %d step(s) failed\",\n            len(failures),\n        )\n        return False\n\n    return not \"\"\"Guard measures the composed string (prompt + embedded), not just the embedded file.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_prompt_plus_embedded_exceeds_limit(self, tmp_path: Path, caplog):\n        \"\"\"File at 90% of _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT plus a large prompt exceeds composed limit.\n\n        Verifies that when prompt + \"\\\\n\\\\n\" + embedded > _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT, a warning\n        is logged but content is _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT bytes.
